rxn_class_summary: Ferrocene-mediated electrochemical difluoromethylarylation of alkynes using CF2HSO2NHNHBoc to construct fluorinated dibenzazepines via a 7-membered ring homolytic aromatic substitution
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ which acts as a single-electron-transfer mediator to oxidize the deprotonated sulfonyl hydrazide (I) to an N-radical (II) and then diazene (III); MeOH is reduced at the cathode to H2 and MeO-

key_intermediates.name: Vinyl radical IV (from CF2H addition to alkyne)
Cyclohexadienyl C-radical V (after 7-ortho cyclization onto arene)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+, which oxidizes the deprotonated sulfonyl hydrazide to give an N-radical and then a diazene that fragments to CF2H radical with loss of SO2/N2; CF2H radical adds to the alkyne to give a vinyl radical, which undergoes 7-ortho cyclization onto the pendant arene, and the cyclohexadienyl radical is reoxidized (by Cp2Fe+) and deprotonated to give the dibenzazepine product.
rate_determining_step: 7-ortho vinyl-radical cyclization onto the arene (HAS, kinetically favored over 6-ipso and 1,5-HAT by DFT)

probe_reagents.name: Vinylcyclopropane radical clock (alkene 49)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 50 bearing CF2H group obtained in 69% yield, evidencing CF2H radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of reagent 1 with base
probe_reagents.observation: Cp2Fe+ oxidizes reagent 1 in presence of Na2HPO4 or LiOMe (more efficient with LiOMe)

probe_reagents.name: Control: no Cp2Fe
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 18% (<5% isolated); mediator essential

probe_reagents.name: Alternative oxidants (tBuOOH/CuSO4; K2S2O8; Ir(ppy)3/hv)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of product 3 under non-electrochemical oxidative conditions, demonstrating necessity of electrochemical mediation

notes: DFT compared three cyclization modes of vinyl radical IV: path a (7-ortho HAS, ΔG = -9.2, ΔG‡ = 10.7), path b (6-ipso, ΔG = -5.4, ΔG‡ = 14.4), and path c (1,5-HAT, ΔG = -13.5, ΔG‡ = 15.6); 7-ortho is kinetically favored. In an undivided cell, Cp2Fe+ may be partially reduced at the cathode, lowering current efficiency. Reagent 1 (CF2HSO2NHNHBoc) is introduced as a new air-stable CF2H radical precursor.
